title: Wallet Import Format (WIF)
description: Wallet Import Format serializes a single Bitcoin private key into a Base58Check string with a network prefix, checksum, and optional compressed-key flag so wallets can export and import raw keys with lower transcription risk.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Wallets
resources.notes: Canonical walkthrough of WIF encoding steps, network prefixes, checksum handling, and the compressed-key suffix.

resources.title: Bitcoin Developer Glossary: WIF
resources.notes: Concise reference that distinguishes WIF from extended private keys and other wallet serialization formats.

resources.title: Mastering Bitcoin
resources.notes: Broad technical reference for private keys, Base58Check encoding, and the legacy wallet import/export formats built on top of them.
